licence.p: This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 Unported Licence. To view a copy of the licence, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ or send a letter to Creative Commons, 444 Castro Street, Suite 900, Mountain View, California, 94041, USA.
Copyright (c) 2019-2025 by Emmanuel Francis & Vincent Tournier.

msContents.summary: Sale of land made by the tēvaraṭiyār of the temple, whose name is lost, to set up an image of Tiruvātavūr-tēvar (Māṇikkavācakar) in the third prakāra of the temple and the food offerings associated with its worship.

handDesc.p: The exact date is given by : November 30th, 1226.
No distinction between e and ē, o and ō.

projectDesc.p: The project DHARMA has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union's Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no 809994).

prefixDef.p: Public URIs with the prefix bib to point to a Zotero Group Library named ERC-DHARMA whose data are open to the public.
Internal URIs using the part prefix to point to person elements in the DHARMA_IdListMembers.xml file.

div.p:
tiripuvaṉa-c cakkaravattikaḷ śrī rājarāja-tēvaṟku yāṇṭu 10 1 Āvatu 10-ṉu-nāyaṟṟu pūrvva-pakṣattu dammiyum tiṅkaṭ-k kiḻamaiyum
peṟṟa Irevati nāḷ

Uyyakkoṇṭār vaḷanāṭṭu ṭu Uṭaiyār tiruviḻimiḻalai Uṭaiyār kōyil muṉṟām pirakārattu Eṭuṅkaruḷi Irukkum tiruvāta
vūr tēvarkku Amutupaṭikku tiru-Amutu Aricikkum ca tukkum Uṭal āka

brahmatēyam tiruviḻimiḻalai meṟpaṭṭa Antaṇaril Āṇṭār cuntara-p perumāṉ vi
rataṅkoṇṭār vaitta kācil Ik-kōyil tēvaraṭiyāru mātam Iṭṭa kācil Eḻāyiratt' aiññūṟu kācu Iṭṭu

Arumoḻitēva-vaḷanāṭṭu Āravala-k-kūṟṟa
ttu nāṭṭiyattāṅkuṭaiyāṉ tiruviḻimiḻalai Uṭaiyāṉ kaḷ tirappāṉ kāṇiyāy Ivaṉ pakkal

Iṉ-ṉāyaṉār tēvatāṉam ceyaṅkoṇṭacōḻa-nal
lūr paṭṭāl vatikku kiḻakku Irāja Irāja vāykkālukku teṟku eḻāṅ kaṇṇāṟṟu mutaṟ catirattu kiḻakkaṭaiya Iṉ-ṉāyaṉār tirunāmattukkāṇi nilam Araiyē araimā
cciṉṉamum niīkki Itaṉ meṟku Iṉ-ṉāyaṉār tirunāmattukkāṇiyāy Ivaṉ vicattiṭai-p parivarttaṉai paṇṇi Ivaṉu tāṉa nilam Oru-māvum Itaṉ meṟku nāṭṭiya
ttāṅkuṭaiyāṉ Eṇṇariya tirunāmam uṭaiyāṉ moṇṇiyāṇṭāṉum pattaraṉṉa paṇṭāramum pakkal Ivaṉ vicattaṭaiya parivattaṉ paṇṇi Ivaṉutā
ṉa nilam Iraṇṭu-māvum Āka nilam muṉṟu-mā

In-nilam muṉṟu-māvum Iṟṟai nāḷāl vilaikoṇṭu Iṉ-ṉāḷ mutal tirunāmattu-k-kāṇi y-āka-k kūṭṭi-k ko
ḷkaiyil Ililattu Iṟaimikutikku I-tiruvātavūr nāyaṉārkku tiru-Amutukku nāḷ Oṉṟukku śrī paṇṭārattē Iru-nāḻi tiru-Amutu Ariciyum Aṟupala-k-kaṟi-y-amutum
Itukku vēṇtum Upp' amutu miḷak' amutu Uḷḷiṭṭa viñcaṉaṅkaḷum Iraṇṭu Aṭaikkāy Amirttum nālu Ilaiyamirtum viṭakkaṭavat' ākavum

periya-tēvar tiripuva
ṉa viīra-tēvarkku-p pattoṉpat' āvatu varaiyil Ic-cuntara-p perumāḷ Ik-kōyil tēvaraṭiyār pakkal Inta tiruvātavūr tēvarkku Amutupaṭikku nilamn
koḷaḷ Irunta kācu y-eṇṇāyiramum Ik-kōyil pattarkaḷ licuvātita tirumaṇṭapa-p paṇikk' uṭal āka

Etirppai ka Ik-kācukku nilaṅ koṇṭu tirunāmattu-k-kā
ṇiyāka-k kūṭṭikkoṇṭu Iṟaimikutikku muṉpu paṇṭārattu It-tiruvātavūrar tēvarkku tiru-Amutupaṭikku viṭṭu varukiṟa nāḷ Oṉṟukku tiru-Amutu Arici Aṟunā
ḻiyum kaṟi Amutu vañcaṉamum Aṭaikkāy Amutukkum Ilai Amutu maṟṟu ṭṭu varukiṟa paṭiye viṭakkaṭavat 'ākavum muṉpu tēvaraṭiyār peṟum
paṭi-c cōṟṟil piṭittu civa-p-pirāmaṇar Aḷantu Amutupaṭi ceytu varukiṟa Arici Iru-nāḻiyum Iṟṟai-nāḷ mutal tavir-k-kaṭavat' ākavum

Ippaṭi cammati
ttu kalveṭṭiṉa-k kuruttamaikku Aruḷāl kōyil kaṇakku kuṉṟan Antaṇa-p-piriyaṉ eḻuttu
Ivai śrī māheśvara kaṇkāṇi tiruviīti e vi Eḻuttu .
11th year of glorious king Tribhuvana Cakravarti Rājarāja, on the Revatī day which is a Monday of the tenth day of the fortnight of the tenth month i.e., Mithunā month.

For the purpose of for the rice offering tiruvamutu aricikkum, for the food offerings given to Tiruvātavūr-tēvar who is graciously seated in the third prakāra of the temple of the Lord of Tiruviḻimiḻalai, located in the Uyyakoṇṭār-vaḷanāṭu.

The record may be best assigned to Rājarāja III. Already ARIE gives the exact date as 30 November 1226, which agrees with the eleventh regnal year of Rājarāja III. Moreover, the inscription itself refers back to the nineteenth year of Periya-tēvar Tribhuvana-Vīratēvar, i.e. a common epithet of king Kulōttuṅga III, under whom Sundara Perumāḷ had already bough lands from the temple tēvaraṭiyār for the food offerings given to Tiruvātavūr-tēvar. Thus, the present record seems to be a recalibration or regularization of a previous arrangement done some thirty years back.
This edition by Renato Dávalos, based on ASI transcript kindly supplied by the Director of Epigraphy in Mysore (2025).
